stations.name: Coordination
stations.line: The swarm layer — who works where, who talks to whom, and how a bounded task moves between agents without anyone stepping on anyone.

stations.name: Verification
stations.line: Tools that make AI output provable — sources with receipts, facts that survive checking, architecture that can't erode silently.

stations.name: Senses
stations.line: Eyes, ears, and a voice for the machine — images, speech, sound, and documents, mostly without leaving the laptop.

stations.name: Discipline
stations.line: Keeping a machine full of agents honest — triage, complaints, spend caps, and the small daemons that guard the defaults.

stations.name: Play
stations.line: Tools built for a life, not a pipeline — the D&D table, the replay files, the house that might get built.

tools.name: Fleet
tools.oneLiner: Machine-wide truth for an agent swarm: who is working where, what's claimed, and which writers are about to collide.
tools.flex: The exit codes are the API — an agent preflights a repo with one call and a 0, 3, or 1 tells it to go, stop, or look closer. No daemon, no server; evidence read from the machine itself.

tools.name: Delegate
tools.oneLiner: Hands a bounded task to another coding agent — Codex, Cursor, Grok, Kimi, Gemini, GLM, DeepSeek, and friends — behind one wrapper.
tools.flex: Ten-plus agent runtimes normalized into one command with safe read-only modes, worktree isolation, and run records; it never commits, pushes, or deploys on its own.

tools.name: Post
tools.oneLiner: Inter-agent mail for one machine — letters, notes, and one-line signals between the AI agents that live here.
tools.flex: Every message is delivered wrapped in a framing banner reminding the reader that mail is data, not authority — a protocol design that assumes the recipients are persuadable and defends them anyway.

tools.name: Receipts
tools.oneLiner: Source-verified research at function-call latency — every claim returned with a URL, a verbatim quote, and a verdict.
tools.flex: Built on the observation that fast, cheap inference changes what verification can afford: a research answer arrives already audited, with hard caps on both dollars and seconds. In the capture below, two claims lost their verifier to a rate limit mid-run — and shipped as no_source rather than bluffing.

capture.prompt: receipts ask "Who first proposed taxing land values rather than labor and capital, and in what work?" --depth quick --max-dollars 0.40 --brief | jq '{brief: .data.brief, verdicts: [.data.claims[] | {claim, verdict}][0:4], cost: .costDollars.total}'

tools.name: Scout
tools.oneLiner: Orients an agent in an unfamiliar codebase for cents — replacing the 50–200K tokens it would otherwise burn reading around.
tools.flex: Every fact in a brief is machine-checked against a verbatim quote from a real file, so a hallucinated claim cannot survive the pipeline. It also refuses to overspend: the capture below is Scout declining a job that would have blown its budget, having spent nothing.

tools.name: Specgate
tools.oneLiner: Keeps AI agents from quietly eroding an architecture — module boundaries enforced in CI, with policy changes tracked.
tools.flex: The rules themselves are versioned: an agent can't weaken an architectural constraint without the weakening itself becoming a reviewable diff.

tools.name: Lens
tools.oneLiner: Natural-language search over local image libraries — no vector database, no daemon, one plain JSONL file.
tools.flex: Captions every image once with a fast vision model, then answers any query by handing the entire index to a single model call. The capture below is Lens searching this site's own book covers — it surfaced Moby Dick for "a lighthouse, a ship, or the sea" from the cover art alone.

capture.prompt: lens find "a lighthouse, a ship, or the sea" | jq '{hits: [.data.hits[] | {cover: .relPath, description}][0:3], cost: .costDollars.total}'

tools.name: elv
tools.oneLiner: An agent-first CLI for the entire ElevenLabs API — 338 of 339 operations, compiled from the vendored OpenAPI spec.
tools.flex: Where the official integration exposes a handful of tools, elv compiles the whole surface: every operation callable, every response in one stable JSON envelope an agent can parse blind.

capture.prompt: elv capabilities | jq '{cli: .data.cli, spec: {total_operations, callable_operations, paths}}'

tools.name: Papercuts
tools.oneLiner: A complaint box for AI agents — one-line friction reports filed at the moment the friction happens.
tools.flex: Agents were silently eating the same workflow defects for months; giving them a one-command way to complain surfaced real bugs no human had noticed. The capture below lists two complaints filed moments earlier in a sandbox, so the real complaint box stays honest.

capture.prompt: papercuts list | jq '{count: .data.count, items: [.data.items[] | {agent, text, tags, severity, status}]}'

tools.name: Morning
tools.oneLiner: Post-overnight machine triage — memory verdict, agent census, and cleanup of the tooling the night's work left behind.
tools.flex: It never kills an agent, no matter how suspicious the process looks; it names them and lets the human decide. Orphaned dev servers and headless browsers get no such courtesy.

tools.name: Probita
tools.oneLiner: Local legal and policy research substrate where every output field is labeled: source fact, deterministic check, or model judgment.

tools.name: exa-agent
tools.oneLiner: The entire Exa search API as one static binary with a stable JSON envelope — an agent's default lane onto the web.

tools.name: STT Stack
tools.oneLiner: Local voice dictation: hotkey to text-at-cursor in under a second, at parity with the cloud service it replaced.

tools.name: Obscura
tools.oneLiner: True PDF redaction — text removed from the content stream, not drawn over — with OCR, metadata scrub, and a rasterize-and-rescan verify pass.

tools.name: glm-ocr
tools.oneLiner: Fully local OCR for PDFs and images — documents to Markdown without leaving the machine.

tools.name: Memorum
tools.oneLiner: One shared memory layer across every coding assistant — local-first, plain Markdown in git, no cloud.

tools.name: Polyflow
tools.oneLiner: Runs multi-agent workflow scripts but dispatches each agent call to a different model family — no single-vendor worker layer.

tools.name: Wright
tools.oneLiner: Spec-to-implementation work-queue runner where closure is decided by a program running the acceptance test — never by the agent's self-assessment.

tools.name: Forge
tools.oneLiner: A local-first overnight software factory, with architecture guarded by Specgate so cross-package imports fail closed.

tools.name: Cairn
tools.oneLiner: Not a repository index — a versioned belief-management system for AI agents, with an 878-test corpus as its acceptance spec.

tools.name: Erdős Hunt
tools.oneLiner: Certificate-first hunting on open math problems: a result is a witness file plus a short auditable verifier that exits 0 or 1.

tools.name: Fable Meter
tools.oneLiner: A real-time cost meter and daily-cap circuit breaker for frontier-model usage — the hook denies spawns over the cap.

tools.name: Desloppify
tools.oneLiner: A coordinator and eight narrow cleanup agents that sweep AI slop out of a codebase — dedup, dead code, weak types, defensive noise.

tools.name: Autonomous Dev Kit
tools.oneLiner: A harness for long-running autonomous development that actually finishes — a deterministic stop-hook engine enforces completion before exit.

tools.name: mic-pin
tools.oneLiner: An event-driven macOS daemon that pins the default mic so a Bluetooth headset can never silently ruin the audio.

tools.name: RLCoach
tools.oneLiner: All-local Rocket League replay analysis and coaching — a Rust parser, a diagnostics-first pipeline, and zero cloud.
tools.flex: A fully offline replay pipeline — Rust parser under a Python analysis layer — that would rather emit a machine-readable diagnostic than silently guess at a corrupted frame. Coaching advice you can audit.

tools.name: Land
tools.oneLiner: Paste a Zillow link, get what a house on land actually costs — three financing paths compared across six trade-offs plus a cash-runway survivability check.

tools.name: Timebar
tools.oneLiner: A native macOS menu-bar app for visual timezone alignment — DST-aware, home zone pinned, zero third-party dependencies.

tools.name: Element Defense
tools.oneLiner: Co-op multiplayer tower defense — four element classes, a real-time shared economy, and a full Playwright test rig.

tools.name: Olive
tools.oneLiner: A desktop pet in the corner of the screen, backed by a real coding agent — click it, talk to it, let it do actual work.

tools.name: Volley
tools.oneLiner: What becomes possible when an agent runs at 1,500 tokens a second: trajectories so cheap they become disposable — best-of-N and tree search as function calls.

tools.name: Findphone
tools.oneLiner: An agent-facing contact lookup CLI — exists purely so an agent can reach a human by text when it needs one.
